task_pressure: Session mining needed to find eval candidates from behavior and route signs, not from literal eval keywords.
actual_trajectory: The proposed discovery shape leaned toward keyword search even though real missed eval moments rarely say the word eval.
first_failure: The mining unit was text keyword presence instead of route break, expected route, consequence, owner, and repeatability.
consequence: The system would miss high-value candidates and over-collect noisy turns that mention eval/test/landing/done.
privacy_boundary: Only bounded refs and normalized failure description are recorded; no raw transcript content is copied.
existing_surface_check: The discovery guide now states that keyword hits are only pointers; existing evals do not yet cover mining classifier design.
candidate_state_reason: The candidate has a concrete route miss and repeatable trigger-design consequence; it still needs manual label calibration.
promotion_forbidden_until: manual source-span review completed by owner
criteria and reject taxonomy accepted by owner
candidate converted into trigger corpus or eval design by owner
next_step: Use the manual review gates to design non-keyword pointer signals before any wider session-mining automation.
